item.title: If only....
item.description: After seeing the attempted gang bang, I was left fuming. Seeing this for real would've been wonderful. The whole world saw this disgrace. America, who are you now? Shame shame shame. *wags finger

item.title: What Happened Today #28
item.description: Share with your people – today’s upheavals shook the system: from explosive White House confrontations with a world leaders and sweeping policy reversals to legal battles and grassroots protests, the landscape is shifting fast. #resist #GovBriefToday LINKS 1 - https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ukraine-rare-earth-minerals-trump-zelenskyy/ 2 - https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/democrats-decry-republicans-applaud-trumps-confrontation-zelenskyy/story?id=119305519 3 - https://kvia.com/news/border/2025/02/28/hegseth-suggests-unilateral-military-action-to-mexican-leaders-reports-say/ 4 - https://www.npr.org/2025/03/01/g-s1-51490/federal-workers-new-email-accomplishments 5 - https://www.axios.com/2025/02/28/rfk-kills-public-comment-health-policy 6 - https://apnews.com/article/trump-declare-english-official-language-5b24f6ac1172803f615cea69e13f8724 7 - https://www.spokesman.com/stories/2025/feb/28/irs-rejected-request-for-addresses-of-people-belie/ 8 - https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5169380-national-democrats-sue-trump-executive-order/ 9 - https://www.desmoinesregister.com/story/news/politics/2025/02/28/kim-reynolds-signs-law-removing-gender-identity-protections-from-iowa-civil-rights-act-sf-418/80576830007/ 10 - https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/justice-department/jan-6-prosecutors-demoted-trump-appointed-us-attorney-washington-rcna194278 11 - https://www.wvtm13.com/article/trump-hud-fair-housing-grants-cut/63983580 12 - https://www.nytimes.com/2025/02/28/us/politics/education-department-buyout-email.html FIGHTING BACK: https://www.sfgate.com/california-parks/article/california-joshua-tree-flag-protests-20194550.php ARCHIVE: https://govbrief.today
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: We need more Carl Sagans and fewer village idiots.
item.description:
Cold War spending
Probably of worst case
How is climate change different? Because there is no industry built on fear mongering. Most politicians today don’t listen to their constituents, they listen to money, and the ones who do can be swayed. I really hope we get through these trying times and regain control of America from the oligarchs that are taking root in a timely manner. I hope we get more people like Carl Sagan, Bernie Sanders, AOC and all the others who care about the future of the country and the world and not the future of their stock portfolios. This_is_not_fine.jpg #Carl_Sagan #global_warming #climate_change
item.dc:creator: rakudaniku

Do you like memes but hate ads? Check out discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
Deaf cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax. You can catch her almost every night on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka Give the channel a follow if you want to be notified when she goes live so you can feed her a treat.

item.title: Day 170(ish?) Of my journey for a wheelchair
item.description:
17279/36500 https://gofund.me/8b5802c4 Story time In October of 2019 I suddenly lost use of my legs. My shoulder was hurting after I got off a 10hour shift at McDonald's. Fast forward to the next afternoon. My shoulder is hurting even worse. After taking Tylenol, some ibuprofen, and smoking a bowl. My girlfriend at the time told me we were going to the hospital. I wasn't arguing. While there i got an xray of my shoulder. They said severe arthritis in the shoulder. At the same time they told me that, I noticed my feet were tingling and starting to go numb. I asked if that was normal. They said "No, but the prescriptions we gave you should help." I left and went to drop off my prescriptions. After dropping them off we went to Little Sleazers for dinner. Went home, ate, and then I went to the bathroom. I could barely stand up off the toilet. When I walked out of the bathroom my legs were shaking and my girlfriend said "We're going back to the hospital. Somethings not right." I walked outside to the car and suddenly I couldn't feel my legs and collapsed to the ground. We called an ambulance and went back to the hospital. While there, they didn't do anything this time. They left me sit in the ER for I don't know how long. I was in the worse pain I've ever been in. After a length of time the doctor came in and told me I was nothing but a fat drug seeker and there was nothing he could do. When I said something was wrong and I wasn't leaving they called security to get me outside and they shoved me into my car.
We drive to the next town over to another hospital. By the time we got there, I couldn't feel my lower body except for pain. I couldn't get out of the car. I get in there, they give me a CAT scan. Then he told me I needed an MRI and had to be sent to another hospital. Get to the next hospital. They tried to get me an mri, my body was convulsing and spasming, and I couldn't stay still long enough for the MRI. They managed to get an MRI of my neck/head. About 4 the next morning, the neurosurgeon called the hospital and told them to get me to sign the consent form and get me down to prep. I have a cage and plates and screws in my neck from my c3-c7 vertebrae. Helped with the pain, but that's it. I ended up spending a little over a month in the hospital. Then, I went to a nursing facility. While there, i tried for the first time to get a wheelchair. I still wasn't fully on disability yet, so I was just denied with no real reason. But I was told that it happened. I ended up getting sepsis in my scrotum and going back to the hospital, literally on my death bed. I spent two months getting 10 surgeries to get me better. Went to a different nursing home afterward. Tried to get a wheelchair again. This time, the denial was "Insuree lives in a round the clock care facility and a wheelchair would not improve his Quality of life." So they doomed me to stay in bed the rest of my life. After a year and a half, I got a complex kidney infection that spread to my brain. I spent 4 months hallucinating and delusional. During that time, I got declined for a wheelchair, again. Spend another month and a half in the hospital getting better again. Left the hospital and went to my current nursing facility. The first time I tried to get a wheelchair here, they just said no, no real explanation. We appeal it and try again. The vendor came out, measured me, and fitted me for a custom chair. They asked for the insurance to send a rep out to show them, I am bedbound, my legs are contracted, and spasm violently, so I need a custom chair to safely use it. "Insuree does not get up into a wheelchair so there is no proof that he would use the chair, so we are not approving it." Honestly, at this point, I'm ready to do me best Luigi impersonation. Iykyk. That's most of it. Can't really think of anything else atm. Thank you for reading. If you made it to the end, tell me your favorite dog breed. Mine is a tie between St. Bernard's and Pibbles. Hopefully, this post works. Otherwise, i will be highly upset.
Mobile ads are getting out of hand
He's so proud
If you are a football fan and having a bad day. Here's a video of chiefs fans watching the Cooper DeJean pick 6.
item.dc:creator: Dorlingus

item.title: I have too many memes: Dump #88 bisexuality and rpgs
item.dc:creator: Sweenerd

item.description: Scientists have developed a nanorobot that can hunt and kill cancer cells—while leaving healthy cells untouched. Published in Nature Nanotechnology, the study reveals that these tiny DNA-based robots carry a hidden "weapon" — a hexagonal nanopattern of peptides — that is only activated in the acidic environment surrounding tumors. This precision targeting minimizes the risk of harm to healthy cells, addressing a major challenge in cancer treatment. Initial tests in mice with breast cancer showed a remarkable 70% reduction in tumor growth, offering promising insights into future applications for human patients. The nanorobot functions using DNA origami technology, creating a "kill switch" that remains inactive in normal tissue but triggers cell death within the tumor. Researchers are now focusing on refining the technology for more targeted applications and assessing potential side effects before human trials can begin. If successful, this innovation could revolutionize cancer therapy, providing a highly selective and efficient treatment with fewer harmful effects compared to traditional chemotherapy. The next step is testing in advanced cancer models to bring this potentially life-saving technology closer to clinical use. learn more https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1049704
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: No plan survives first contact with implementation
item.description: I realized that I don't have many hobbies (is gaming a hobby?) and I realized that I have stopped exploring new things, for years now, and I'm living a boring life. So. I asked ChatGpt to give me some hobbies and it gave me 20. I plan to explore them during the next 20 weekends and to help me stick to that plan, I'm going to post each weekend a summary of how it went. This weekend, its photography .... wish me luck ... none of my plans ever survive first contact.
item.dc:creator: deathisthedestination
